sections.name: 1. Schema Conformance
checks: storyType is one of the 11 enum values
primaryAngle is a non-empty string
secondaryAngle is a non-empty string distinct from primary
readerPromise is a non-empty string
targetAudience is an array with ≥ 1 item from the 8-value enum
knowledgeGap is a non-empty string
editorialQuestions is an array with exactly 5 items
Each editorialQuestion ends with ?
storyDepth is one of the 5 enum values
recommendedModules is an array of valid module names

sections.name: 2. Story Discovery (Step 1)
checks: Primary angle answers 'why is this happening?' not just 'what happened?'
Angle identifies a systemic issue, not just a surface event
Angle identifies who benefits and who loses
Angle identifies what changed

sections.name: 3. Angle Generation (Step 2)
checks: ≥ 5 possible angles were generated (traceable in reasoning)
Primary and secondary angles are distinct categories (e.g. Economic vs Political)
Angles are ranked, not randomly selected

sections.name: 4. Story Type & Depth (Steps 3 & 8)
checks: Story type is explicitly chosen and justified by the evidence
Story depth matches story type (e.g. Investigation → Long-form+)
Opinion type is only used if explicitly requested

sections.name: 5. Reader Promise & Audience (Steps 4 & 5)
checks: Reader promise is specific (not 'you'll learn about X')
Reader promise is honest and deliverable with available evidence
Target audience is explicitly classified from the controlled list
Audience detection is justified by topic and angle

sections.name: 6. Knowledge Gap (Step 6)
checks: Knowledge gap identifies a specific misconception or unknown
Knowledge gap is not trivial ('people don't know about this')
If no genuine gap exists, story is flagged as unnecessary

sections.name: 7. Editorial Questions (Step 7)
checks: Exactly 5 editorial questions
Questions are distinct, not rephrasings of each other
Questions cover who, what, why, how, and what next
Every question can be answered by available evidence
Questions define the scope — every module answers at least one

sections.name: 8. Module Recommendations (Step 9)
checks: Only relevant modules are included (no filler)
Module list is not the default set — tailored to the story
Minimum 3 modules, maximum 8
Hero module is included (visual anchor required)

sections.name: 9. Quality Gate
checks: ≥ 5 story angles generated and ranked
Reader promise is specific, honest, and deliverable
Target audience explicitly identified from controlled list
Knowledge gap clearly stated — flag if no gap exists
Exactly 5 editorial questions from ≥ 20 generated
Story depth matches complexity and evidence quality
Only relevant modules recommended (no filler)
Story type explicitly chosen, not defaulted
No writing — output is strategic, not editorial content
Primary angle is distinct from secondary angle
